title: philadelphus incanus var. incanus
intro: Philadelphus incanus var. incanus, also known as the Alpine Mock Orange, is a shrubby perennial plant that belongs to the Hydrangeaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Philadelphus incanus var. incanus include its ability to reduce fever, ease coughs, and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
